testPrincipalSeeding.method: JWT principals minted in memory from tenant-specific Vault signing keys; no token material was written to disk or evidence.

notes: FLOW-13 role analysis marks tenant-admin, event-organiser, freelancer, business-partner, and platform-admin as full analytics roles.
Tenant-user and platform-support are partial role cells and are still represented in the seeded role map.
Runtime auth probe route is GET /api/runs/phase0-auth-probe/trace with declared roles tenant-user, tenant-admin, and platform-admin.
Valid FLOW-13 auth probes used tenant-admin because it is an active FLOW-13 role and accepted by the runtime route.
Wrong-role probe used platform-support: a FLOW-13 partial support role that is intentionally denied on the selected protected route.

The initial POST /api/flow/execute probe was not used for final evidence because successful authorization enters the AI generation executor and can exceed a short HTTP auth-probe timeout; the guard behavior is covered by the lightweight protected run-trace route.

liveAuthMatrix.name: valid acme tenant-admin token in acme tenant
valid northwind tenant-admin token in northwind tenant
valid tessera tenant-admin token in tessera tenant
acme token in northwind tenant denied
northwind token in tessera tenant denied
acme token in tessera tenant denied
wrong platform-support role denied on run trace

verification.serverTypecheck: Push-Location server; npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json --pretty false; Pop-Location: exit 0.
verification.serverJest: Push-Location server; npx jest --testPathPatterns=data-warehouse-analytics --runInBand --colors=false; Pop-Location: 2 suites passed, 63 tests passed.

commands: docker compose -f docker-compose.platform.yml up -d vault returned an existing container-name conflict; docker start xiigen-vault reused the existing dev Vault container
docker exec -e VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200 -e VAULT_TOKEN=<redacted> xiigen-vault vault status -format=json
docker exec ... vault kv put secret/xiigen/tenants/{tenant}/github_token value=<redacted>
docker exec ... vault kv put secret/xiigen/tenants/{tenant}/auth/jwt_signing_key value=<redacted> algorithm=HS256
POST http://localhost:33001/api/tenants for acme-corp, northwind, tessera-collective
Live HTTP auth matrix against http://localhost:33001/api/runs/phase0-auth-probe/trace using curl status codes
FLOW-13 phase-close scan: throwNew=0, clsImports=0, servicesMissingMicroserviceBase=0, directTenantContextImports=0, directSdkImports=0, anyTypes=0
notes: Vault Docker health reports unhealthy for the existing dev container, but vault status returned sealed=false and all KV readbacks succeeded.
Tokens were minted in memory from tenant-specific Vault signing keys for HTTP verification and were not written to evidence, docs, or env files.
PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest returned NullReferenceException for successful protected GET responses in this shell; final matrix used curl.exe status codes to avoid that client bug.
Cross-tenant failures are structural JWT failures because the server verifies with the context tenant key before checking the token tenant claim.
No tenant credentials, JWTs, or signing keys were printed, copied to docs, or committed.
